title: ranunculus ficaria subsp. chrysocephalus
intro: Ranunculus ficaria subsp. chrysocephalus, also known as lesser celandine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of lesser celandine include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds. Lesser celandine can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or cream, with the latter often used topically to treat skin conditions and the former taken internally to promote overall well-being.
